author: @gerryplayz4532
text: On the bright side it means that there's lots of people who really don't want you to get injured while making content for us, I think some of the extra warnings in comments probably help young or new people who missed what seems obvious 😎👍

author: @frankbenevolence
text: I think the formalization of health and safety is mostly to prevent employers from forcing unnecessary risk on their staff because it's faster or cheaper.   It gives a legal framework to challenge unsafe business practices.  Sadly, if you don't make it a rule, many employers won't provide a safety conscious environment for their workers.

author: @stuartbarker9373
text: Victims of domestic violence commonly claim in hospital that their injuries were caused by walking into doors, cupboards, etc. So that's what gets recorded. When numbers seem absurd, it's probably best to investigate how the absurdity arose before assuming the numbers are accurate.

author: @AlienLivesMatter
text: Cowardly hiding from risk for fear of encountering it is less worthwhile to practising managing it successfully

author: @marcfruchtman9473
text:
One of the greatest risks for me is to watch day after day and not to proceed with doing anything.
I do agree that we need to make personal responsibility more important than it currently is. So I totally agree with you there. I  think the big difference is that Videos are often "visited" by anyone... with no prerequisites and of unknown age group as well as unknown experience.  When I am presenting material in a Formalized Setting, I have the luxury of a "Captive Audience", and they are required to pass the "Safety" test... otherwise I won't let them proceed with "physical experiments" or.. perhaps I make it my business to over-supervise that individual. But with just a video topic, there is not really any prerequisite safety video... and so a lot of people just don't get that there are dangers to doing stuff... with batteries, electricity, windmills etc... And yea, they do need to take personal responsibility for learning the risks. 
   Having said that, I wouldn't mind seeing a message in the "Description" that says "This video may contain potentially dangerous activity and do the research of the risks and dangers before attempting anything on your own, as well as to proceed at your own risk." (or something similar). That wouldn't require re-uploading or editing the video... and it pretty much covers the issues. (Of course, I don't know the rules in the UK, so, consult your legal on your end too) heheh 

You have a huge number of videos, so you might want to get a trusted individual to do such a thing to help you with the time factor.

author: @selfcorrected-Bobby
text: nice cybernetics experiment on the chimps. Learned behavior may have its limitations apparently. Much like mimicry an indeed, ARTIFICIAL 'intelligence'.Merely a simulacrum of intelligence if at that stage it is a learned behavior, not an invented one. However now I'm getting worried about what AI they say will be capable of, in inventing things, by making correlations that people were not able to make. Perhaps given enough time, we would have though. AI is just quanta od data rehierarchicalisation, not qualification to any sort of higher order whatsoever, in my opinion. How about Qualitum Physics? Not Quantum Physics, hey? C'mon now! Now but really, people think the AI will actually be sentient?! Or, Conscious? It will simply be an apparition, a simulacrum whose intelligence surpasses ours. Yet there will be no actual sentience, only the literal  pretense of it, as a simulacrum. The chimps learned, but their learning gave them alzheimers as they never thought any further..
